pages.transcription:
Agreement

made this 28th day of December 1938, by and between
STEFAN ZWEIG
whose address is 49 Hallam Street, London, W. 1, England

the Author and/or Proprietor, party of the first part (hereinafter in either case called the "Author"),
and THE VIKING PRESS, INC., of 18 East 48th Street, New York, N. Y., party of the second part
(hereinafter called the "Publishers"), respecting a work
at present entitled "BEWARE OF PITY"

(hereinafter called "Said Work"), whereby it is mutually agreed between the Author and the Publishers
as follows:

PART ONE

1. The Author agrees to deliver the finished work to the Publishers
and hereby grants and assigns to the Publishers the sole and exclusive right to publish said work in
volume form in the English language in the United States of America and Canada
and also grants to the Publishers such further rights as are specified in Paragraph 5 hereafter. The phrase
"in volume form" shall be defined as meaning publication of the work as a whole, or substantially com-
plete, as a unit or at one time.

2. The Publishers agree to publish said work in volume form at their own expense, in the style or
styles and at the price or prices which they deem best suited to its sale, within nine months from the
date of receipt of the finished manuscript, but they shall not be responsible for delays caused by strikes,
fires, or other circumstances beyond their control.

3. The Publishers shall take all steps which may be necessary to take out copyright in the name of
The Viking Press, Inc. and thereby secure their own rights and those of the Author
under the United States Copyright Acts, and shall take out renewals whenever they fall due, if the copy-
right is in their name; but if the copyright is in the name of the Author, he agrees to take out such
renewals.

4. The Publishers agree to pay the Author as follows:
catalogue retail price
(a) A royalty of 15 per cent of the catalogue retail price for all copies
sold of said work, less copies returned, with no deduction for cash discounts or bad debts,

with the following exceptions:
(b) On all copies sold for export at a reduced price, and on all copies sold in quantities to justify
50 sales to wholesale book dealers
a special discount of 50 per cent or more from the catalog retail price, such as sales to Canada,
or sales in bulk to recognized Book Clubs or Reading Circles, a royalty of 7 1/2 per
catalogue retail price
cent of the amount which the Publishers charge, as qualified above; but if said work is sold
to a recognized Book Club which pays an outright sum for a license to publish said work
for distribution to its members or subscribers, the Author shall receive 50 per cent of the
amount received by the Publishers;

(c) On copies of any text-book edition at a retail price lower than that of the regular trade edi-
tion, issued for sales in bulk to educational institutions, a royalty of 10 per cent of the amount
which the Publishers charge, as qualified above;
pages.notes: Die Transkription folgt dem Layout des Dokuments. Handschriftliche Korrekturen (Zahlen/WĂ¶rter) wurden an den entsprechenden Stellen eingefĂŒgt.

pages.transcription:
(d) On copies of overstock which the Publishers, after one year from the first publication of the
said work, deem it expedient to sell at "remainder" prices, i.e., at less than one-half the
catalog retail price, a royalty of 10 per cent of the amount which the Publishers charge, except
that when such copies are sold at or below cost, no royalty shall be paid;

(e) On all copies sold of any cheap edition which they publish, after one year from the first
publication of said work, at a price substantially less than the original retail price, a royalty
of 10 per cent of the amount which the Publishers charge, as qualified above; but if the
Publishers shall arrange for the publication of such cheap or reprint edition by another pub-
lisher, the Author shall receive 50 per cent of the amount received by the Publishers;

(f) If the Publishers cede to an English or other foreign publisher the right to publish an edition
in English of said work on a royalty basis or for an outright sum, the amount of such income
shall be divided per cent to the Author and per cent to the
Publishers. If the Publishers sell to an English or other foreign publisher an edition of said
work in sheets or bound at a price inclusive of royalty, the Author shall receive
per cent of the amount received by the Publishers.

5. The additional rights which the Author grants exclusively to the Publishers in Paragraph 1 above,
and the division of any and all sums accruing from the sale of these rights, are as follows:
(a) Reprinting in whole or in part in newspapers, magazines, anthologies and other volumes,
second serialization, radio-broadcasting, and mechanical reproduction, 50 per
cent to the Author and 50 per cent to the Publishers;
(b) Translation, per cent to the Author and per cent to the Publishers;
(c) First serialization, per cent to the Author and per cent to the Publishers;
(d) Motion picture and/or dramatic rights, 85 per cent to the Author and 15 per cent
to the Publishers. It is agreed that the Publishers are to receive
the stipulated share only on a sale or sales made through them.

6. The Author hereby grants to the Publishers the option to publish the Author's next
book-length work on the terms of the present agree-
ment.
The publishers shall exercise this option within two months of receipt of the completed manuscript,
except that they shall not be required to exercise it until at least one month after the publication of
the next preceding book, and they shall publish the new book within one year of the date of its ac-
ceptance. If one of the option books should not be accepted, the Publishers' option on subsequent
books shall become null and void.

All rights not herein specifically granted to the Publishers are reserved by the Author.

PART TWO

1A. Default in delivery of manuscript by the date above stated may be deemed cause for the termination of
this agreement if the Publishers should so elect, and, in such case, they shall have the right to recover from the
Author any and all amounts which they may have advanced to him; but should the Publishers consent to accept
the manuscript at a later date, they shall not be required to publish within the limit of time above stated.

2A. If an index is required or if drawings, photographs, tables, maps, charts or other illustrative matter
should be deemed necessary by both parties, the copy shall be prepared or supplied by the Author or, in his
default, by the Publishers at his expense. If the Author make alterations in the proof which cost more than 10
per cent of the cost of composition, exclusive of the cost of correcting printers' errors, he agrees to pay such excess.
pages.notes: Das Dokument enthĂ€lt handschriftliche Durchstreichungen (X-Markierung ĂŒber dem Textblock in der Mitte). Diese wurden als solche transkribiert.

layout_regions.label: Randnotizen zu den Zusatzrechten in Abschnitt 5

pages.transcription:
3A. The Author covenants and represents that he is the sole author and proprietor of the said work, that he
has full power to make this agreement and grant, that it in no way infringes upon the copyright or proprietary
right of others, and that it contains no libelous or other unlawful matter. The Author agrees to indemnify and
hold harmless the said Publishers from all manner of claims, demands, suits or proceedings which may be asserted
or instituted on the ground that the said work violates any copyright or proprietary right, or that it contains any-
thing libelous or unlawful; and further agrees to indemnify and hold the Publishers harmless from all charges,
liability, costs, expenses or damages of any kind or nature whatsoever arising to the Publishers, or which the
Publishers may incur or sustain by reason of any of the foregoing. In case of any infringement of the copy-
right the Publishers may in their discretion sue or employ such remedies as they deem expedient, and all such
suits or proceedings shall be at the joint expense of Author and Publishers, and the net proceeds of any recovery
shall be divided equally between them, but the Author shall not be liable for any expenditure in excess of $300.00
undertaken by the Publishers without his present consent in writing.

4A. The Author agrees that he will not, without the written permission of the Publishers, publish or permit
to be published in book or pamphlet form, while this agreement is in force, any material based on said work,
or of such a nature as to impede or reduce its sale.

5A. If the Author should arrange for publication of said work elsewhere than in the United States (pro-
vided Paragraph 1 does not preclude him from doing so), he agrees that it shall not precede publication in the
United States without the Publishers' consent in writing; and that, if publication elsewhere has taken or will
take place with the Publishers' consent, they shall not be responsible for securing American copyright if it should
be impracticable to obtain it without publishing sooner than agreed as above.

6A. If, at the time of publication of a cheap or reprint edition, the Publishers adjust the price of copies of
the regular edition remaining unsold in the hands of booksellers, the royalty on such copies shall be adjusted
to conform to the terms for the cheap edition as stated above.

7A. On copies furnished gratis to the Author, or for review, advertising, sample or like purposes, or on
copies destroyed by fire or water, no royalty shall be paid.

8A. The Publishers may publish, or permit others to publish, without payment, such selections from said
work for publicity purposes as they may consider appropriate to benefit its sale.

9A. The Publishers shall give to the Author on publication 10 free copies of said work, and he shall have
the right to purchase copies for personal use, but not for re-sale, at 60 per cent of the catalog retail price.

10A. The Publishers agree to render semi-annual statements of account to April 30 and October 31 of each
year during August and February following, and to make settlement in cash at the same time. Whenever the
semi-annual earnings fall below $10.00 no accounting or payment shall be made until the next settlement date
after the earnings have aggregated $10.00, unless requested in writing by the Author. Should the Author receive
an overpayment he agrees that the Publishers may deduct such amount from any further earnings of said work
or any other books by him which may be published by the Publishers; it being understood that an unearned
advance is not an "overpayment."

11A. If the Publishers should fail to pay moneys due or to deliver statements as agreed, within three months
from the date of written demand by the Author or his representatives, this agreement shall be cancelled and all
rights in said work granted herein shall revert to the Author forthwith, and without further notice, and without
prejudice to moneys due to him from the Publishers.

12A. Should the Publishers fail to keep said work in print and for sale and after written demand from the
Author decline or neglect to reprint within three months and to offer it for sale; or in case after two years from
the date of first publication they give three months' notice to the Author of their desire and intention to discon-
tinue publication, this agreement shall terminate and all rights granted hereunder shall revert to the Author,
together with any existing property originally furnished by him at his expense. In such event the Author shall
have the right to purchase the plates, if any, of said work at one-half of the manufacturing cost (including com-
position) and/or any remaining copies or sheets at one-half of the manufacturing cost, same, exclusive of over-
head, in default of which the Publishers shall dispose of the same as they may see fit, without prejudice to moneys
due to the Author.

This agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, executors, administrators or assigns of the Author,
and upon the successors or assigns of the Publishers, but no assignment shall be binding on either of the
parties without the written consent of the other party to this agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties hereto have hereunto set their hands and seals the day and
year first above written.
IN THE PRESENCE OF:

____________________ Witness

____________________ Witness

Stefan Zweig [Unterschrift]
Author and/or Proprietor
THE VIKING PRESS, INC.
By Ben Huebsch [Unterschrift]
pages.notes: Die Unterschriften wurden als solche gekennzeichnet. Die violette Tinte von Stefan Zweig ist deutlich erkennbar.

made this 28th day of December 1938 , by and between
STEFAN ZWEIG
whose address is 49 Hallam Street, London, W. 1, England

the Author and/or Proprietor , party of the first part (hereinafter in either case called the "Author"),

plete, as a unit or at one time.

styles and at the price or prices which they deem best suited to its sale, within nine months from the
date of receipt of the finished manuscript, but they shall not be responsible for delays caused by strikes,
fires, or other circumstances beyond their control.

Copyright

The Viking Press, Inc. and thereby secure their own rights and those of the Author
under the United States Copyright Acts, and shall take out renewals whenever they fall due, if the copy-

export and

said work, deem it expedient to sell at "remainder" prices, i.e., at less than one-half the

(e) On all copies sold of any cheap edition which they publish, after one year from the first

ment.

pages.transcription_a:
3A. The Author covenants and represents that he is the sole author and proprietor of the said work, that he
has full power to make this agreement and grant, that it in no way infringes upon the copyright or proprietary
right of others, and that it contains no libelous or other unlawful matter. The Author agrees to indemnify and
hold harmless the said Publishers from all manner of claims, demands, suits or proceedings which may be asserted
or instituted on the ground that the said work violates any copyright or proprietary right, or that it contains any-
thing libelous or unlawful; and further agrees to indemnify and hold the Publishers harmless from all charges,
liability, costs, expenses or damages of any kind or nature whatsoever arising to the Publishers, or which the
Publishers may incur or sustain by reason of any of the foregoing. In case of any infringement of the copy-
right the Publishers may in their discretion sue or employ such remedies as they deem expedient, and all such
suits or proceedings shall be at the joint expense of Author and Publishers, and the net proceeds of any recovery
shall be divided equally between them, but the Author shall not be liable for any expenditure in excess of $300.00
undertaken by the Publishers without his present consent in writing.

4A. The Author agrees that he will not, without the written permission of the Publishers, publish or permit
to be published in book or pamphlet form, while this agreement is in force, any material based on said work,
or of such a nature as to impede or reduce its sale.

5A. If the Author should arrange for publication of said work elsewhere than in the United States (pro-
vided Paragraph 1 does not preclude him from doing so), he agrees that it shall not precede publication in the
United States without the Publishers' consent in writing; and that, if publication elsewhere has taken or will
take place with the Publishers' consent, they shall not be responsible for securing American copyright if it should
be impracticable to obtain it without publishing sooner than agreed as above.

6A. If, at the time of publication of a cheap or reprint edition, the Publishers adjust the price of copies of
the regular edition remaining unsold in the hands of booksellers, the royalty on such copies shall be adjusted
to conform to the terms for the cheap edition as stated above.

7A. On copies furnished gratis to the Author, or for review, advertising, sample or like purposes, or on
copies destroyed by fire or water, no royalty shall be paid.

8A. The Publishers may publish, or permit others to publish, without payment, such selections from said
work for publicity purposes as they may consider appropriate to benefit its sale.

9A. The Publishers shall give to the Author on publication 10 free copies of said work, and he shall have
the right to purchase copies for personal use, but not for re-sale, at 60 per cent of the catalog retail price.

10A. The Publishers agree to render semi-annual statements of account to April 30 and October 31 of each
year during August and February following, and to make settlement in cash at the same time. Whenever the
semi-annual earnings fall below $10.00 no accounting or payment shall be made until the next settlement date
after the earnings have aggregated $10.00, unless requested in writing by the Author. Should the Author receive
an overpayment he agrees that the Publishers may deduct such amount from any further earnings of said work
or any other books by him which may be published by the Publishers; it being understood that an unearned
advance is not an "overpayment."

11A. If the Publishers should fail to pay moneys due or to deliver statements as agreed, within three months
from the date of written demand by the Author or his representatives, this agreement shall be cancelled and all
rights in said work granted herein shall revert to the Author forthwith, and without further notice, and without
prejudice to moneys due to him from the Publishers.

12A. Should the Publishers fail to keep said work in print and for sale and after written demand from the
Author decline or neglect to reprint within three months and to offer it for sale; or in case after two years from
the date of first publication they give three months' notice to the Author of their desire and intention to discon-
tinue publication, this agreement shall terminate and all rights granted hereunder shall revert to the Author,
together with any existing property originally furnished by him at his expense. In such event the Author shall
have the right to purchase the plates, if any, of said work at one-half of the manufacturing cost (including com-
position) and/or any remaining copies or sheets at one-half of the manufacturing cost, same, exclusive of over-
head, in default of which the Publishers shall dispose of the same as they may see fit, without prejudice to moneys
due to the Author.

This agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, executors, administrators or assigns of the Author,
and upon the successors or assigns of the Publishers, but no assignment shall be binding on either of the
parties without the written consent of the other party to this agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties hereto have hereunto set their hands and seals the day and
year first above written.
IN THE PRESENCE OF:

____________________ Witness

____________________ Witness

Stefan Zweig [Unterschrift]
Author and/or Proprietor
THE VIKING PRESS, INC.
By Ben Huebsch [Unterschrift]

pages.transcription:
AGREEMENT made this first day of March, 1939, between STEFAN
ZWEIG of 49 Hallam Street, London, W. 1, England, and THE VIKING PRESS, INC.
of 18 East 48th Street, New York City, U.S.A.:

1. The Publishers agree to publish in a single volume
"ADEPTS IN SELF-PORTRAITURE" and "THREE MASTERS" (Balzac, Dickens,
Dostoeffsky) for both of which the Publishers now possess the world rights
in the English language, together with an English version of "DER KAMPF MIT
DEM DAMON," all three of which works are by said Stefan Zweig who hereby
agrees to give said Viking Press all rights in the last named work that
they now possess in the first two named.

2. It is agreed between the Publishers and the Author that
the terms, conditions, rights and obligations under which the above named
volume shall be issued, are to be in all respects identical with the agree-
ment between the same parties dated December 28, 1938 with respect to
"BEWARE OF PITY" by the same author, but with the following exceptions:

(a) The Author grants and assigns to the Publishers the
sole and exclusive right to publish said work in all
countries; and

(b) The Publishers agree to pay to the Author fifteen
(15%) per cent of the amount that they may receive for
editions for export; and

(c) The Publishers agree to pay the author eighty (80%)
per cent of such income as they may derive from the
sale of the right to an English or other foreign publisher
to publish an edition in English on a royalty basis or
for an outright sum.

3. The purpose of the above paragraphs "a" and "b" is
to make the present agreement conform to the Publishers' present rights in
"ADEPTS IN SELF-PORTRAITURE" and "THREE MASTERS", and the purpose of
paragraph "c" is to provide for a contingency that is not covered by the
BEWARE OF PITY agreement.

4. The Author agrees to supply to the Publishers an
adequate English version of "DER KAMPF MIT DEM DAMON" at a reasonable
price and this version shall remain the sole and exclusive property of
the Publishers.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have hereunto set their
hands and seals the day and year first above written.

Stefan Zweig
AUTHOR
THE VIKING PRESS INC.
B. W. Huebsch
Vice-President
pages.notes: Die Unterschrift von Ben Huebsch wurde als 'B. W. Huebsch' transkribiert, da sie im Original als 'B. W. Huebsch' lesbar ist.

adequate English version of "DER KAMPF MIT DEM DAMON" at a reasonable
price and this version shall remain the sole and exclusive property of
the Publishers.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have hereunto set their
hands and seals the day and year first above written.

Stefan Zweig
Author

B W Huebsch

V e r t r a g

Zwischen Herrn Dr. Stefan Zweig, Kapusinerberg, 5, Salzburg, Oes-
terreich, einerseits, und der Firma Editorial Juventud S.A., Pro-
venza, 101, Barcelona, Spanien, andererseits, wurden folgende Ver-
einbarungen getroffen:

1.- Herr Dr. Stefan Zweig uebertraegt der Firma Editorial Juven-
-- Juventud, S.A., das alleinige Uebersetzungsrecht fuer die spa-
nische Sprache an den Buechern:

"CASTRELIO GEGEN CALVIN "
(Kampf um einen Scheiterhaufen)

& "Kaleidoscope" (Novellenband)

fuer alle Auflagen und Ausgaben.

2.- Die Firma Editorial Juventud verpflichtet sich Herrn Dr.
-- Zweig sechs Exemplare der spanischen Ausgaben als Beleg so-
fort nach Erscheinen im Buchhandeln zu ueberweisen.

3.- Die Firma Editorial Juventud hat Herrn Dr. Stefan Zweig fuer
-- alle verkauften Exemplare obiger Buecher eine Tantieme von
zehn (10) Prozent zu zahlen, auf den Ladenpreis berechnet. Als Ga-
rantie fuer diese Tantieme zahlt Editorial Juventud Herrn Dr.
Zweig einen Vorschuss von fuenfhundert Reichsmark bei Unterzeich-
nung dieses Vertrages.

4.- Sollte ein Vorabdruck oder Nachdruck der spanischen Ueberset-
-- zung in Zeitungen oder Zeitschriften erfolgen, so sind[?] falls
es sich nicht um Reklameabdruck handelt, fuenfzig Prozent der Er-
loese an Herrn Dr. Zweig bei Eingang der Betraege abzufuehren.

5.- Falls die Firma Editorial Juventud die Werke ausgehen laesst,
-- ohne einen Neudruck innerhalb eines Jahres zu veranstalten,
so wird die Uebersetzung Eigentum des Herrn Dr. Zweig.

6.- Abrechnungen ueber die verkauften Exemplare hat zweimal im
-- Jahre, und zwar am 1.Januar und 1.Juli, zu erfolgen; nach
diesen Abrechnungen faellig werdende Betraege sind innerhalb sech-
sig Tage an Herrn Dr. Zweig zu leisten.

7.- TextĂ€nderungen duerfen nur mit Einverstaendnis Herrn Doktor
-- Zweigs gemacht werden.

Obige Abmachungen wurden zum Zeichen des Einverstaendnis von
beiden Teilen unterzeichnet.

Barcelona, den 9.Juli, 1936
EDITORIAL JUVENTUD, S. A.
El Director Gerente,
[Unterschrift unleserlich]

Barcelona, den 15. Juli 1936

Stefan Zweig

MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT made this 30th day of No-
vember of 1945 between Dr. Manfred Altmann and Mrs. Hannah
Altmann, of 109 Regency Lodge, London, N.W. 3, for them-
selves, their successors and assigns of the one part,
and EDITORIAL JUVENTUD S. A., of Provenza, 101, Barcelona,
Spain, for themselves, their successors and assigns of the
other part. Dr. Manfred Altmann and Mrs. Hannah Altmann
are the heirs and executors of the late Stefan Zweig, and
as such they sign the present contract. It is hereby agreed
between the parties as follows:--

1. Dr. and Mrs. Altmann grant to Editorial Juventud S. A.
the right to translate and publish in the Spanish language
the work by the late Stefan Zweig entitled:

DIE WELT VON GESTERN,

with no limit to the number of copies.------------------------

2. Editorial Juventud S. A. shall forward to Dr. and Mrs.
Altmann six complimentary copies of the Spanish edition
of the said work on publicarion.------------------------------

3. Editorial Juventud S. A. shall pay to Dr. and Mrs. Alt-
mann on the signing hereof the sum of 400 (four hundred)
dollars on account and in anticipation of the royalty of
ten per cent. calculated on the published price of the
above mentioned work.------------------------------------------

4. Editorial Juventud S. A. shall deliver to Dr. and Mrs.
Altmann within three months of the 30th June and the 31st
December respectively of each year a statement of the num-
ber of copies sold of the Spanish edition during the six
months before the preceding 30th June and 31st December res-
pectively, and shall at the time of the delivery of the said
statements pay any royalties due.------------------------------

5. If, after the expiration of three years from the date of
first publication of the Spanish edition the Buyers shall
allow the said work to go out of print and remain out of
print for one year then in such case all rights conveyed in
this agreement shall revert to Dr. and Mrs. Altmann.--------

AS WITNESS the hands of the parties:--

EDITORIAL JUVENTUD, S. A.
El Director Gerente

4/. Die Bestimmung der AuflagenhĂ¶he, der Ausstattung und der Festsetzung

6/. Die zwischen dem Autor und dem Herbert Reichner Verlag Wien-ZĂŒrich-
Leipzig abgeschlossenen VertrĂ€ge ĂŒber die im Herbert Reichner Verlag Wien-

tragen. Das Hauptwerk des Autors soll auf diese Weise in angemessener Aus-
stattung bei dem Verlag erscheinen und wo mĂ¶glich zu einer Ă€usserlich ein-
heitlichen Gesamtausgabe vereinigt werden.
Der Verlag verpflichtet sich, die " Sternstunden der Menschheit " oder ein

pages.transcription:
Anekdoten ĂŒber St. Z.

Der schwer aussprechbare Name

Stefan Zweig hat das gerade fĂŒr einen international so bekannten Autor peinliche Missgeschick, einen Namen zu besitzen, der in den meisten Sprachen schwer aussprechbar ist. Die englische Sprache kennt soviel wie gar nicht das Z, die franzĂ¶sische nicht das W. Das I bereitet so ziemlich allen Sprachen ausser der deutschen besondere Schwierigkeiten, und so ist es kein Wunder, dass Stefan Zweig seinen Namen in den verschiedenen LĂ€ndern in den verschiedensten Formen zu hĂ¶ren bekommt. Am verhĂ€ngnisvollsten ist diese Verwirrung am Telephon, und das fĂŒhrte jĂŒngst zu einer heiteren Episode. Ein franzĂ¶sischer Freund wollte telegraphisch seine Ankunft in London anzeigen und gab oder versuchte vielmehr das Telegramm telephonisch aufzugeben. SelbstverstĂ€ndlich verstand das TelephonfrĂ€ulein nicht den Namen; er wiederholte ihn einmal, zweimal, dreimal mit mĂ¶glichster Deutlichkeit - vergebens. Schliesslich begann er nach dem Code zu buchstabieren: Z gleich Zebra, W gleich William... Das TelephonfrĂ€ulein verstand noch immer nicht ganz. Er wiederholte nochmals. Endlich kommt ein Seufzer der Erleichterung von dem geplagten und anscheinend literarisch sehr gebildeten TelephonfrĂ€ulein: "Ach, also ganz wie Stefan Zweig, der berĂŒhmte Autor!"

__________________________________________________________________________

Der Schutzheilige der Friseure

Stefan Zweig hat viele Leser in der ganzen Welt. Aber seine getreuesten AnhĂ€nger sind merkwĂŒrdigerweise die Friseure, und zu dieser Liebe ist er auf eine sehr sonderbare Weise gekommen. Stefan Zweig liess sich im Hotel bei einem Aufenthalt an der Riviera tĂ€glich bei einem sehr alten Friseur rasieren, der frĂŒher Friseur der ComĂ©die FranĂ§aise gewesen war, alle mĂ¶glichen PrĂ€sidenten sowie Anatole France und alle denkbaren BerĂŒhmtheiten rasiert hatte. WĂ€hrend des Rasierens wusste er von ihnen die schĂ¶nsten Geschichten auf die charmanteste Weise zu erzĂ€hlen. Aus Dankbarkeit fĂŒr diese vielen reizenden Geschichten, die ihm durch einen Monat hindurch
pages.notes: Die Ăberschrift 'Anekdoten ĂŒber St. Z.' ist handschriftlich hinzugefĂŒgt.

pages.transcription:
2
jeden Morgen zwischen Einseifen und Rasieren erzĂ€hlt wurden, schrieb Stefan Zweig in einer Zeitung ein kleines Portrait dieses Friseurs und erwĂ€hnte darin, wie anregend es fĂŒr einen Schriftsteller und ĂŒberhaupt fĂŒr jeden sei, die Morgentoilette gleichzeitig mit einer Bereicherung des allgemeinen Wissens zu verbinden. Er schrieb, wie in frĂŒheren Zeiten die Friseure und die Friseusen eigentlich die BotengĂ€nger und Zeitungen der frĂŒheren Jahrhunderte waren und wie unergiebig das Selbstrasieren im geistigen Sinne sei, wĂ€hrend dessen man weder nachdenken noch sich richtig erzĂ€hlen lassen kĂ¶nne.

Diese unschuldige Bemerkung fand eine vĂ¶llig unerwartete Resonanz. Eine nach der andern bemĂ€chtigten sich die Friseurzeitungen dieses Aufsatzes, der ihre Kunst und ihren Beruf gegenĂŒber der Maschine des Herrn Gillette wieder zu Ehren brachte. Zwei Friseurinnungen grosser StĂ€dte luden ihn ein, Ehrenmitglied ihres Vereins zu werden, und da man in den Zeitungen auch sein Bild reproduzierte, wurde er seitdem in sĂ€mtlichen FriseurlĂ€den mit besonderer Achtung behandelt. Freilich rasierte ihn kein Friseur mehr ohne zu versuchen, ihm seine ganzen Memoiren zu erzĂ€hlen, sodass ihm zum Schluss in seiner Verzweiflung nichts ĂŒbrig blieb, als gegen seine eigene Ueberzeugung sich einen schweigsamen Rasierapparat anzuschaffen.

__________________________________________________________________________

Der gefĂ€hrliche Titel

Dass Autoren mit einem Buche Regierungen verĂ€rgert oder beunruhigt haben, ist in der Geschichte nichts Neues. Stefan Zweig ist das schon mit einem blossen Titel gelungen. Einer seiner kleinen Romane "The Burning Secret", hat fĂŒr ein paar Stunden eine ganze Stadt und ein ganzes Land erregt.

Schon frĂŒher hatte dieses Buch ein merkwĂŒrdiges Schicksal erfahren. Es war 1916 in der englischen Uebersetzung von einem amerikanischen Verlag erworben worden und sollte gerade erscheinen,

3
als Amerika in den Weltkrieg eintrat. Da schien es dem amerikanischen Verleger einerseits zu bedenklich, ein Buch mit dem Namen eines Ă¶sterreichischen Schriftstellers zu bringen, anderseits hĂ€tte es ihm um die Druckbogen leid getan. So ĂŒbersetzte er rasch den Namen Stefan Zweigs ins Englische und es erschien unter dem Namen "Stephen Branch".

Nach dem Kriege wurde in Deutschland ein Stummfilm daraus gemacht und im Jahre 1933 ein Sprechfilm. Ende Februar 1933 klebte an allen PlakatsĂ€ulen Berlins und in der Provinz das Plakat "The Burning Secret", als plĂ¶tzlich die Nachricht von dem mysteriĂ¶sen Reichstagsbrand in Berlin sich verbreitete. Die deutsche Regierung erklĂ€rte diesen Brand als das Werk von Kommunisten und gestattete keine Ă¶ffentliche Diskussion ĂŒber diese dunkle Angelegenheit. Aber gleich am nĂ€chsten Tage sah man ĂŒberall lĂ€chelnde und schmunzelnde Leute vor den Plakaten "The Burning Secret" stehen, und das Wort bekam eine den oberen Kreisen unerwĂŒnschte PopularitĂ€t. Noch am selben Abend wurden auf polizeilichen Befehl alle Plakate heruntergerissen und der Film, der kaum drei- oder viermal gelaufen war, verboten, sodass dieser gefĂ€hrliche Film (der in Wirklichkeit mit Politik nicht das mindeste zu tun hat) fĂŒr ewige Zeiten aus Deutschland verschwunden ist und erst jetzt in englischer Sprache wieder seine Auferstehung feiern soll.

__________________________________________________________________________

Stefan Zweig ist seit seiner frĂŒhesten Jugend ein leidenschaftlicher Autografensammler gewesen und gilt in den Kreisen der Bibliothekare und der HĂ€ndler als unwidersprechliche AutoritĂ€t, was Echtheit, Seltenheit und Wert von Autografen betrifft. Er wird selbst von Museen Ă¶fters zu Gutachten herangezogen. Ein einzigesmal hat ein HĂ€ndler seine Kenntnisse auf das heftigste bestritten.

4
Das geschah in einer franzĂ¶sischen Provinzstadt. Stefan Zweig sah in der Auslage eines mittleren BuchhĂ€ndlers, von dem er nie gehĂ¶rt hatte, eine Reihe von Autografen ausgestellt, alte und moderne. Er ging in den Laden und liess sich die Sachen zeigen, wĂ€hlte gleich zwei StĂŒcke, die ihm interessant und preiswert schienen. Der HĂ€ndler, mit sĂŒdlichem Temperament begabt, suchte ihm mehr aufzunĂ¶tigen und empfahl dringend jedes einzelne StĂŒck. Unter anderm brachte er auch ein kleines Manuskript von Stefan Zweig, die Antwort auf eine Rundfrage, die dieser an eine Zeitung gegeben hatte, und nannte dafĂŒr einen ziemlich hohen Preis. Stefan Zweig versuchte ihm zu erklĂ€ren, dass die Manuskripte dieses Autors nicht so kostbar seien, weil er zu fleissig arbeite, noch lebe und weiss Gott wie lange noch leben werde. Aber er stiess auf heftigen Widerspruch. Der BuchhĂ€ndler geriet in Erregung. Nein, das sei ein ganz besonders wichtiges Manuskript und er werde auch in Paris gewiss kein so schĂ¶nes mehr auftreiben kĂ¶nnen. Er war so eifrig, alle die literarischen QualitĂ€ten Stefan Zweigs dem Autor selbst zu erklĂ€ren, dass dieser begann, sich zu schĂ€men und gegen seine innere Ueberzeugung sein eigenes Manuskript ĂŒberzahlte und kaufte. Freilich, als dann der BuchhĂ€ndler ihn um seine Adresse bat, um ihm weiterhin seine Kataloge zuschicken zu kĂ¶nnen, gab er vorsichtigerweise einen anderen Namen an.

Monsieur Stefan Zweig,

Cette volontĂ© de parcourir le monde et de jouir

Ă©tonnez ni de la distance, ni du temps. Vos yeux resplendis-
sants d'une hardiesse confiante, penĂštrent aussi les mystĂšres
des villes civilisĂ©es comme les mystĂšres des forĂȘts oĂč habitent

se dĂ©roule dans les gratte-ciel des modernes Babylones ainsi que

tuel de la Reforme.

L'Inde, le Ceylan, la Chine, l'Egypte vous ont donnĂ© satisfaction

dĂ©sertes - tout cela assista au passage du voyageur tranquille.
Tout cela a vu traverser celui qui toujours conserva dans son

Dans ces voyages, combien de fois vous avez ren-

A Paris, oĂč vous habitez pendant une annĂ©e, vous avez gagnĂ©

nement vous a surpris. C'Ă©tait la plus absurde des nouvelles
qui venait assombrir votre esprit de citoyen de toutes les pa-

Votre pays avait besoin de tous les citoyens va-
lides. Vous, Monsieur Stefan Zweig, vous qui aimiez conscien-

ses, vous deviez ĂȘtre mobilisĂ©. Pendant trois annĂ©es, vous avez
Ă©tĂ© sous les armes. Heureusement la destinĂ©e vous a Ă©tĂ© propi-

tĂ© de 1914, l'homme qui - comme une Ăźle de la raison perdue dans

l'esprit suffisait pour marquer une des plus hautes qualitĂ©s de

Monsieur Stefan Zweig, vous ĂȘtes un citoyen de
l'Univers.

Vous etiez encore un adolescent quand vous avez
traduit dans votre langue les poĂšmes de VERLAINE, BAUDELAIRE,

Comme critique et biographe vous avez voyagĂ© dans

Votre curiositĂ© ne se trouve jamais satisfaite!
C'est de la curiositĂ© d'un homme patient, d'un savant, de quel-

-4-

COPIA.
s'amuser en observant les paysages ou restant dans une simple

C'est avec vous Monsieur Stefan Zweig, ma capacitĂ© pour analyser
les esprits n'est pas trop grande. Mais, autant qu'il m'est

le grand sens de la poesie que vous rĂ©vĂ©lez dans tout ce que

l'amour du mystĂšre dans les Ăąmes? Ne serait-ce pas de la poĂ©-
sie la force de l'enthousiasme? Ne serait-ce pas de la poĂ©sie
le sentiment de misericorde pour les faibles, les opprimĂ©s et

-5-

COPIA.
les vaincus? PoĂšte dans le sens de GOETHE - voilĂ  comment vous
a appellĂ© ROMAIN ROLLAND. Le magistral crĂ©ateur de Jean Chris-
tophe a trouvĂ© une formule parfaite pour dĂ©finir votre indivi-

Votre oeuvre, d'auteur de nouvelles reproduit la
grande misericorde pour les opprimĂ©s, pour les fragiles, pour

Ă  tous les genres de souffrances. Les deux extrĂȘmes de la vie
mĂ©ritent votre pitiĂ©. Vous aimez les enfants et Ă  cause de cet

tourments que sont les Ăąmes des garĂ§ons. Vous Ă©prouvez comme
personne les drames qui devastent ces Ăąmes. Vous les montrez
dans la plĂ©nitude de son impĂ©tuositĂ© et de son ardeur. Avec
vous nous apprenons Ă  connaĂźtre le petit Edgard. Il a Ă  peine

la forme de l'Ăąme des multitudes."

Vous le dites, vous que ne l'ignorez point, parce
que vous les avez vu mille fois dans l'histoire et vous les avez
vu dans la vie, les extrĂȘmes tĂ©nebreux auxquels peuvent conduire
la haine quand elle s'abat sur les hommes. Quand le souvenir
de ces pages vous reviendra, vous vous imaginerez que le conti-
nent lequel vous vous trouvez est vouĂ© Ă  ĂȘtre celui dont ait
rĂȘve Erasme et les humanistes - qui est aussi votre rĂȘve - devien-
dra une rĂ©alitĂ©, c'est dans cette espĂ©ir, Monsieur Stefan Zweig,
que l'AcadĂ©mie BrĂ©silienne de Lettres vous adresse sa bienvenue,
au moment oĂč elle a l'honneur de recevoir votre visite.

MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT made this 21st day of October
1937 BETWEEN Herrn Stefan Zweig of 49, Hallam Street,
London, W.1, his executors, administrators and assigns
(hereinafter called the Author) of the one part and
Cassell & Company Limited, of La Belle Sauvage, Ludgate
Hill in the City of London, their successors or assigns
(hereinafter called the Publishers) of the other part.

WHEREAS the said Author has written a new and original
work at present entitled:

"THE BURIED CANDELABRUM"

(hereinafter called the said work) the manuscript of
which is in the hands of the Publishers and which they
are willing to publish in the Autumn of 1937, in Limited
Editions at the published prices of ÂŁ5/5/-d. and 30/-d.
per volume in the first instance NOW IT IS HEREBY AGREED
AS FOLLOWS:

1. The Author hereby grants to the Publishers on the terms
and conditions set forth in this agreement the sole and exclusive
licence to print, publish and sell the said work in book form in
the English language in the United Kingdom of Great Britain, its
Dominions, Colonies and Dependencies (except Canada) and in
Ireland, and on the Continent of Europe (but the Publishers agree
that the Author shall be free to arrange for the publication of
the work in any Continental edition in the English language,
such publication not to take place earlier than nine months after
first publication in England by the Publishers), the rest of
the World except Canada and the United States of America to be
an open market for copies produced in the English language.

2. The Author hereby guarantees that the said work contains
nothing which is a violation of the copyright of any other person
or is of a libellous character and he will hold the Publishers
harmless from all suits, claims and proceedings which may be
brought against them on the ground that the said work is a
violation of copyright or that it contains anything libellous.

3. IN CONSIDERATION HEREOF the Publishers agree to pay the
Author royalty at the following rates on all copies of the said
work sold:

(a). A royalty of Twelve-and-one-half per cent (12Âœ%)
of the published price of copies of both editions
sold.

(b). On copies of the above editions sold Overseas or
in Great Britain for the purpose of Export:
A royalty of Ten per cent (10%) of the price
received on all such copies sold.

(c). On Cheap Editions:
Ten per cent (10%) of the published price of
copies sold.

(d). The Publishers shall pay the Author on the day
of publication in advance and on account of
royalty payable under this agreement a sum
equivalent to the amount of royalty earned on
orders of the said work received by them up to
that date.

Zwischen Libreria Internazionale Sperling & Kupfer
S.A., Mailand, Via Larga 21, im Vertrage "Italienischer Ver-
leger" genannt, und Herrn Stefan Zweig, Salzburg, Kapuzinerberg 5,
im Vertrag genannt "Eigentuemer", wurde heute folgender Vertrag
abgeschlossen:

Â§ 1.
Der Eigentuemer uebertraegt dem italienischen Ver-
leger die Rechte fuer die italienische Buchausgabe im gesamten
italienischen Sprachgebiet seines Werkes
" Die Heilung durch den Geist ".

Â§ 2.
Fuer die Ueberlassung der Buchrechte verpflichtet
sich der italienische Verleger zu einer Vorschusszahlung von
Lire 1000.-- Ă  Conto eines Honorars von 10 % des Ladenpreises
alles festverkauften Exemplare. Die Abrechnung des Honorars
von 10% erfolgt halbjaehrlich und zwar am 1.Juli bezw. 31.De-
zember jedes Jahres.

Â§ 3.
Fuer das Vorabdrucksrecht zahlt der italienische Ver-
leger dem Eigentuemer 50 % aller Eingaenge aus solchen Verkaeu-
fen und verpflichtet sich zur Ueberweisung dieses 50%igen An-
teils innerhalb 14 Tagen nach Empfang solcher Honorare.

Â§ 4.
Besprechungs-und Freiexemplare sind honorarfrei.

Â§ 5.
Der italienische Verleger verpflichtet sich, das Werk
bis Weihnachten 1931 erscheinen zu lassen.

Â§ 6.
Der italienische Verleger verpflichtet sich zur un -
entgeltlichen Ueberlassung von 6 Pflichtexemplaren.

Â§ 7.
Alle andern Rechte als die oben festgelegten Buch-
und Vorabdrucksrechte bleiben ausdruecklich dem Eigentuemer vor-
behalten.

Â§ 8.
Alle Zahlungen unter diesem Vertrag sind zu leisten
an Herrn Stefan Zweig, Salzburg, Kapuzinerberg 5. Der ausschliess-
liche Gerichtsstand fuer Streitigkeiten aus diesem Vertrage ist
Mailand.
Vorstehender Vertrag wurde in zwei gleichen Exemplaren
ausgefertigt von beiden vertragsschliessenden Teilen gelesen,
anerkannt und unterschrieben und geht mit seinen Rechten und
Pflichten auf die Rechtsnachfolger der Vertragsschliessenden
ueber. S. A. SPERLING & KUPFER
Il Presidente
Mailand, den 12. Mai 1931 [?] [?] [?]
Salzburg den 9. Mai 1931 Stefan Zweig

AGREEMENT made this 30th day of March, 1927, between Stefan Zweig
of Salzburg, Austria, and The Viking Press of New York.

I. The AUTHOR grants the sole and exclusive publication rights
in the English language in his version of Ben Jonson's "Volpone"
for all countries.

II. The PUBLISHERS agree to pay the following royalty:

a) On sales of the American edition 10% on the
the first five thousand copies; 12 1/2% on the
second five thousand; and 15% on sales over
ten thousand. The royalty on copies sold
at half the retail price or less shall be
one half the royalty then prevailing.

b) If a British or other foreign edition be
sold on royalty basis, the royalty derived
is to be shared equally between the AUTHOR
and the PUBLISHERS.

c) On any other income to be derived from the
sale of the use of the book in whole or in
part, the net proceeds shall be divided
equally between the AUTHOR and the PUBLISHERS.

III. The AUTHOR shall receive six free copies of the said work.

IV. The PUBLISHERS agree to render semi-annual statements of
account as of April 30th and October 31st of each year, and to
make settlement in cash three months after the respective dates.

V. In case of any infringement of the copyright of said work
the PUBLISHERS may, in their discretion, sue or employ such re-
medies as they may deem expedient, and all such suits or proceed-
ings shall be at the joint expense of the AUTHOR and PUBLISHERS ,
and the net proceeds of any recovery collected shall be divided
equally between the parties hereto.

VI. If after two years from publication the PUBLISHERS wish to
withdraw from this agreement, the AUTHOR shall have the right
upon three months' notice to purchase the plates at half the cost
of production and the stock at cost, and all rights shall revert
to him. Should he fail to exercise the option, the PUBLISHERS
shall have the right to melt the plates.

VII. If after two years from publication the PUBLISHERS shall
allow said work to go out of print and fail to republish upon
three months' notice by the author, the latter shall have the op-
tion to purchase the plates at fifty percent of the cost of pro-
duction and all the rights herein granted shall thereupon revert
to him.

VIII. If after two years from publication the PUBLISHERS determine
that there is no further sale for said work in the regular chan-
nels of the trade, they shall have the right to dispose of the
copies on hand at cost or less without payment of royalty to the
AUTHOR on such copies.

IX. This contract may be assigned by either party, but only with
the consent of the other party to this agreement.

X. It is further agreed that the stipulations and agreements
herein shall apply to and bind the heirs, executors, administra-
tors and assigns of the AUTHOR and the successors and assigns of
the PUBLISHERS.

pages.transcription:
Agreement

made this 30th day of June 19 36, by and between
Stefan Zweig
whose address is Kapuzinerberg 5, Salzburg, Austria
49 Hallam street, W.1. London England
the Author and/or Proprietor , party of the first part (hereinafter in either case called the "Author"),
and THE VIKING PRESS, INC., of 18 East 48th Street, New York, N. Y., party of the second part
(hereinafter called the "Publishers"), respecting a work
at present entitled " THE RIGHT TO HERESY: Castellio Against Calvin "

(hereinafter called "Said Work"), whereby it is mutually agreed between the Author and the Publishers
as follows:

PART ONE

1. The Author agrees to deliver the finished work to the Publishers by (already received)
and hereby grants and assigns to the Publishers the sole and exclusive right to publish said work in
volume form in the English language throughout the world

and also grants to the Publishers such further rights as are specified in Paragraph 5 hereafter. The phrase
"in volume form" shall be defined as meaning publication of the work as a whole, or substantially com-
plete, as a unit or at one time.

2. The Publishers agree to publish said work in volume form at their own expense, in the style or
styles and at the price or prices which they deem best suited to its sale, within nine months from the
date of receipt of the finished manuscript, but they shall not be responsible for delays caused by strikes,
fires, or other circumstances beyond their control.

3. The Publishers shall take all steps which may be necessary to take out copyright in the name of
The Viking Press, Inc. and thereby secure their own rights and those of the Author
under the United States Copyright Acts, and shall take out renewals whenever they fall due, if the copy-
right is in their name; but if the copyright is in the name of the Author, he agrees to take out such
renewals.

4. The Publishers agree to pay the Author as follows:
(a) A royalty of 15 per cent of the catalog retail price for all copies
sold of said work,

with the following exceptions:
(b) On all copies sold for export at a reduced price, and on all copies sold in quantities to justify
a special discount of 50 per cent or more from the catalog retail price, such as sales to Canada,
or sales in bulk to recognized Book Clubs or Reading Circles, a royalty of 7 1/2 per
cent of the catalog retail price, but if said work is sold
to a recognized Book Club which pays an outright sum for a license to publish said work
for distribution to its members or subscribers, the Author shall receive 50 per cent of the
amount received by the Publishers;

(c) On copies of any text-book edition at a retail price lower than that of the regular trade edi-
tion, issued for sales in bulk to educational institutions, a royalty of 10 per cent of the amount
which the Publishers charge, as qualified above;
pages.notes: Typoskript mit handschriftlichen ErgĂ€nzungen und Korrekturen. Die Seite ist gut lesbar.

pages.transcription:
PART TWO

1A. Default in delivery of manuscript by the date above stated may be deemed cause for the termination of
this agreement if the Publishers should so elect, and, in such case, they shall have the right to recover from the
Author any all amounts which they may have advanced to him; but should the Publishers consent to accept
the manuscript at a later date, they shall not be required to publish within the limit of time above stated.

2A. If an index is required or if drawings, photographs, tables, maps, charts or other illustrative matter
should be deemed necessary by both parties, the copy shall be prepared or supplied by the Author or, in his
default, by the Publishers at his expense. If the Author make alterations in the proof which cost more than 10
per cent of the cost of composition, exclusive of the cost of correcting printers' errors, he agrees to pay such excess.

All rights not herein specifically granted to the Publishers are reserved by the Author.

5. The additional rights which the Author grants exclusively to the Publishers in Paragraph 1 above,
and the division of any and all sums accruing from the sale of these rights, are as follows:

(a) Reprinting in whole or in part in newspapers, magazines, anthologies and other volumes,
second serialization, syndication, radio-broadcasting, and mechanical reproduction, 50 per
cent to the Author and 50 per cent to the Publishers;

(c) First serialization, 80 per cent to the Author and 20 per cent to the Publishers;
(d) Motion picture and/or dramatic rights, 85 per cent to the Author and 15 per cent
to the Publishers.

(d) On copies of overstock which the Publishers, after one year from the first publication of the
said work, deem it expedient to sell at "remainder" prices, i.e., at less than one-half the
catalog retail price, a royalty of 10 per cent of the amount which the Publishers charge, except
that when such copies are sold at or below cost, no royalty shall be paid;

(e) On all copies sold of any cheap edition which they publish, after one year from the first
publication of said work, at a price substantially less than the original retail price, a royalty
of 10 per cent of the catalog retail price of such edition, but if the
Publishers shall arrange for the publication of such cheap or reprint edition by another pub-
lisher, the Author shall receive 50 per cent of the amount received by the Publishers;

(f) If the Publishers cede to an English or other foreign publisher the right to publish an edition
in English of said work on a royalty basis or for an outright sum, the amount of such income
shall be divided 85 per cent to the Author and 15 per cent to the
Publishers. If the Publishers sell to an English or other foreign publisher an edition of said
work in sheets or bound at a price inclusive of royalty, the Author shall receive 15
per cent of the amount received by the Publishers.
pages.notes: Typoskript mit handschriftlichen Korrekturen. Die Seite ist gut lesbar.

pages.transcription:
3A. The Author covenants and represents that he is the sole author and proprietor of the said work, that he
has full power to make this agreement and grant, that it in no way infringes upon the copyright or proprietary
right of others, and that it contains no libelous or other unlawful matter. The Author agrees to indemnify and
hold harmless the said Publishers from all manner of claims, demands, suits or proceedings which may be asserted
or instituted on the ground that the said work violates any copyright or proprietary right, or that it contains any-
thing libelous or unlawful; and further agrees to indemnify and hold the Publishers harmless from all charges,
liability, costs, expenses or damages of any kind or nature whatsoever arising to the Publishers, or which the
Publishers may incur or sustain by reason of any of the foregoing. In case of any infringement of the copy-
right the Publishers may in their discretion sue or employ such remedies as they deem expedient, and all such
suits or proceedings shall be at the joint expense of Author and Publishers, and the net proceeds of any recovery
shall be divided equally between them, but the Author shall not be liable for any expenditure in excess of $300.00
undertaken by the Publishers without his previous consent in writing.

4A. The Author agrees that he will not, without the written permission of the Publishers, publish or permit
to be published in book or pamphlet form, while this agreement is in force, any material based on said work,
or of such a nature as to impede or reduce its sale.

5A. If the Author should arrange for publication of said work elsewhere than in the United States (pro-
vided Paragraph 1 does not preclude him from doing so), he agrees that it shall not precede publication in the
United States without the Publishers' consent in writing; and that, if publication elsewhere has taken or will
take place with the Publishers' consent, they shall not be responsible for securing American copyright if it should
be impracticable to obtain it without publishing sooner than agreed as above.

6A. If, at the time of publication of a cheap or reprint edition, the Publishers adjust the price of copies of
the regular edition remaining unsold in the hands of booksellers, the royalty on such copies shall be adjusted
to conform to the terms for the cheap edition as stated above.

7A. On copies furnished gratis to the Author, or for review, advertising, sample or like purposes, or on
copies destroyed by fire or water, no royalty shall be paid.

8A. The Publishers may publish, or permit others to publish, without payment, such selections from said
work for publicity purposes as they may consider appropriate to benefit its sale.

9A. The Publishers shall give to the Author on publication 10 free copies of said work, and he shall have
the right to purchase copies for personal use, but not for re-sale, at 60 per cent of the catalog retail price.

10A. The Publishers agree to render semi-annual statements of account to April 30 and October 31 of each
year during August and February following, and to make settlement in cash at the same time. Whenever the
semi-annual earnings fall below $10.00 no accounting or payment shall be made until the next settlement date
after the earnings have aggregated $10.00, unless requested in writing by the Author. Should the Author receive
an overpayment he agrees that the Publishers may deduct such amount from any further earnings of said work
or any other books they may be published by the Publishers; it being understood that an unearned
advance is not an "overpayment."

11A. If the Publishers should fail to pay moneys due or to deliver statements as agreed, within three months
from the date of written demand by the Author or his representatives, this agreement shall be cancelled and all
rights in said work granted herein shall revert to the Author forthwith, and without further notice, and without
prejudice to moneys due to him from the Publishers.

12A. Should the Publishers fail to keep said work in print and for sale and after written demand from the
Author decline or neglect to reprint within three months and to offer it for sale; or in case after two years from
the date of first publication they give three months' notice to the Author of their desire and intention to discon-
tinue publication, this agreement shall terminate and all rights granted hereunder shall revert to the Author,
together with any existing property originally furnished by him at his expense. In such event the Author shall
have the right to purchase the plates, if any, of said work at one-half of the manufacturing cost (including com-
position) and/or any remaining copies or sheets at one-half of the manufacturing cost of same, exclusive of over-
head, in default of which the Publishers shall dispose of the same as they may see fit, without prejudice to moneys
due to the Author.

This agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, executors, administrators or assigns of the Author,
and upon the successors or assigns of the Publishers, but no assignment shall be binding on either of the
parties without the written consent of the other party to this agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties hereto have hereunto set their hands and seals the day and
year first above written.

IN THE PRESENCE OF:

__________________________
Witness

__________________________
Witness

Stefan Zweig
Author and/or Proprietor

THE VIKING PRESS, INC.
By __________________________
Vice-President

other work

book-length work

6. The Author hereby grants to the Publishers the option to publish the Author's next
book-length work on the terms of the present agree-
ment.

All rights not herein specifically granted to the Publishers are reserved by the Author.

Additional
rights:

5. The additional rights which the Author grants exclusively to the Publishers in Paragraph 1 above,
and the division of any and all sums accruing from the sale of these rights, are as follows:

(a) Reprinting in whole or in part in newspapers, magazines, anthologies and other volumes,
second serialization, syndication, radio-broadcasting, and mechanical reproduction, 50 per
cent to the Author and 50 per cent to the Publishers;

(b) Translation, ~~per cent to the Author and~~ ~~per cent to the Publishers;~~
(c) First serialization, ~~per cent to the Author and~~ ~~per cent to the Publishers;~~
(d) Motion picture and/or dramatic rights, 85 per cent to the Author and 15 per cent
to the Publishers.

(d) On copies of overstock which the Publishers, after one year from the first publication of the
said work, deem it expedient to sell at "remainder" prices, i.e., at less than one-half the
catalog retail price, a royalty of 10 per cent of the amount which the Publishers charge, except
that when such copies are sold at or below cost, no royalty shall be paid;

(e) On all copies sold of any cheap edition which they publish, after one year from the first
publication of said work, at a price substantially less than the original retail price, a royalty
catalogue retail price of such edition
of 10 per cent of the ~~amount which the Publishers charge, as qualified above;~~ but if the
Publishers shall arrange for the publication of such cheap or reprint edition by another pub-
lisher, the Author shall receive 50 per cent of the amount received by the Publishers;

(f) If the Publishers cede to an English or other foreign publisher the right to publish an edition
in English of said work on a royalty basis or for an outright sum, the amount of such income
shall be divided per cent to the Author and per cent to the
Publishers. If the Publishers sell to an English or other foreign publisher an edition of said
work in sheets or bound at a price inclusive of royalty, the Author shall receive
per cent of the amount received by the Publishers.

3A. The Author covenants and represents that he is the sole author and proprietor of the said work, that he
has full power to make this agreement and grant, that it in no way infringes upon the copyright or proprietary
right of others, and that it contains no libelous or other unlawful matter. The Author agrees to indemnify and
hold harmless the said Publishers from all manner of claims, demands, suits or proceedings which may be asserted
or instituted on the ground that the said work violates any copyright or proprietary right or that it contains any-
thing libelous or unlawful; and the Author agrees to indemnify and hold the Publishers harmless from all charges,
liability, costs, expenses or damages of any kind or nature whatsoever arising to the Publishers, or which the
Publishers may incur or sustain by reason of any of the foregoing. In case of any infringement of the copy-
right the Publishers may in their discretion sue or employ such remedies as they deem expedient, and all such
suits or proceedings shall be at the joint expense of Author and Publishers, and the net proceeds of any recovery
shall be divided equally between them, but the Author shall not be liable for any expenditure in excess of $300.00
undertaken by the Publishers without his previous consent in writing.

4A. The Author agrees that he will not, without the written permission of the Publishers, publish or permit
to be published in book or pamphlet form, while this agreement is in force, any material based on said work,
or of such a nature as to impede or reduce its sale.

5A. If the Author should arrange for publication of said work elsewhere than in the United States (pro-
vided Paragraph 1 does not preclude him from doing so), he agrees that it shall not precede publication in the
United States without the Publishers' consent in writing; and that, if publication elsewhere has taken or will
take place with the Publishers' consent, they shall not be responsible for securing American copyright if it should
be impracticable to obtain it without publishing sooner than agreed as above.

6A. If, at the time of publication of a cheap or reprint edition, the Publishers adjust the price of copies of
the regular edition remaining unsold in the hands of booksellers, the royalty on such copies shall be adjusted
to conform to the terms for the cheap edition as stated above.

7A. On copies furnished gratis to the Author, or for review, advertising, sample or like purposes, or on
copies destroyed by fire or water, no royalty shall be paid.

8A. The Publishers may publish, or permit others to publish, without payment, such selections from said
work for publicity purposes as they may consider appropriate to benefit its sale.

9A. The Publishers shall give to the Author 10 free copies of said work and he shall have
the right to purchase copies for personal use, but not for re-sale, at 60 per cent of the catalog retail price.

10A. The Publishers agree to render semi-annual statements of account to April 30 and October 31 of each
year during August and February following, and to make settlement in cash at the same time. Whenever the
semi-annual earnings fall below $10.00 no accounting or payment shall be made until the next settlement date
after the earnings have aggregated $10.00, unless requested in writing by the Author. Should the Author receive
an overpayment he agrees that the Publishers may deduct such amount from any further earnings of said work
or any other books by him which may be published by the Publishers; it being understood that an unearned
advance is not an "overpayment."

11A. If the Publishers should fail to pay moneys due or to deliver statements as agreed, within three months
from the date of written demand by the Author or his representatives, this agreement shall be cancelled and all
rights in said work granted herein shall revert to the Author forthwith, and without further notice, and without
prejudice to moneys due to him from the Publishers.

12A. Should the Publishers fail to keep said work in print and for sale and after written demand from the
Author or decline to reprint within three months and to offer it for sale; or in case after two years from
the date of first publication they give three months' notice to the Author of their desire and intention to discon-
tinue publication, this agreement shall terminate and all rights granted hereunder shall revert to the Author,
together with any existing property originally furnished by him at his expense. In such event the Author shall
have the right to purchase the plates, if any, of said work at one-half of the manufacturing cost (including com-
position) and/or any remaining copies or sheets at one-half of the manufacturing cost of same, exclusive of over-
head, in default of which the Publishers shall dispose of the same as they may see fit, without prejudice to moneys
due to the Author.

This agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, executors, administrators or assigns of the Author,
and upon the successors or assigns of the Publishers, but no assignment shall be binding on either of the
parties without the written consent of the other party to this agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties hereto have hereunto set their hands and seals the day and
year first above written.
IN THE PRESENCE OF:

[Unterschrift Lotte Altmann] Witness
[Unterschrift Rita Whiteson] Witness

[Unterschrift Stefan Zweig] Author and/or Proprietor
By [Unterschrift Ben Huebsch] THE VIKING PRESS, INC.

Bestimmungen fĂŒr die Verwendung der Scheckvordrucke.
1. Auf dem mittleren Teil (eigentlichen Scheck) sind immer Ort, Tag, Monat und Jahr der Ausstellung anzugeben. Vorausdatierung ist verboten. Bleibt in den fĂŒr die Angabe der Geldsummen bestimmten Zeilen ein unbeschriebener Raum, so muĂ dieser mit einem starken waagrechten Strich ausgefĂŒllt werden.
2. Auf dem Abschnitt fĂŒr den EmpfĂ€nger ist immer der Betrag in Ziffern anzugeben. Mitteilungen an den ZahlungsempfĂ€nger kĂ¶nnen auf diesem Abschnitt mit dem gewĂŒnschten Erfolg nur dann angebracht werden, wenn es sich um eine einzelne Ăberweisung auf ein anderes Scheckkonto oder auf ein auslĂ€ndisches Postscheckkonto, um eine einzelne Barzahlung durch ein Ă¶sterreichisches Postamt oder um eine einzelne Zahlung mit Wertbrief handelt, weil nur in diesen FĂ€llen der Abschnitt dem ZahlungsempfĂ€nger zukommt.
3. Der Buchungszettel ist bei Namenschecks auszufĂŒllen, bei andern Schecks (Kassen-, Erlagschein-, Dauer-, Auslandchecks) abzutrennen.
4. Bei der Ausstellung eines Kassenschecks sind auf dem mittleren Teil des Scheckvordrucks die Zeilen zwischen dem Betrag und der Unterschrift des Ausstellers leer zu lassen. Auf dem Abschnitt fĂŒr den EmpfĂ€nger ist nur der Betrag in Ziffern anzugeben. Der Buchungszettel ist abzutrennen.
5. Auf Namenschecks ist der ZahlungsempfĂ€nger im mittleren Teil des Scheckvordrucks und im Buchungszettel anzugeben. Die Anschrift des ZahlungsempfĂ€ngers muĂ auf dem mittleren Teil des Scheckvordrucks so bestimmt angegeben werden, daĂ jeder UngewiĂheit vorgebeugt wird. Die vollstĂ€ndige Anschrift ist auch notwendig, wenn der Scheckaussteller den Betrag an sich selbst anweist; Angaben wie âan mich selbstâ, âan eigene Orderâ oder dgl. genĂŒgen nicht. Im Buchungszettel kann die Bezeichnung des EmpfĂ€ngers verstĂ€ndlich gekĂŒrzt werden. (Zuname, Wohnort.)
6. Ăberweisungsverkehr. Hat der ZahlungsempfĂ€nger ein Konto beim Postsparkassenamt, so wird der Betrag des Schecks vom Konto des Scheckausstellers abgeschrieben und auf dem Konto des EmpfĂ€ngers gutgeschrieben, wenn nicht auf dem mittleren Teil des Scheckvordrucks bei der Bezeichnung des ZahlungsempfĂ€ngers durch den Vermerk âzur Barzahlungâ ausdrĂŒcklich die bare Auszahlung des angewiesenen Betrags verlangt ist. Ist die Nummer des Scheckkontos des EmpfĂ€ngers dem Aussteller bekannt, so ist sie auf allen drei Teilen des Scheckvordrucks anzugeben. Der Abschnitt fĂŒr den GutschriftsempfĂ€nger wird diesem mit dem Kontoauszug zugesendet.
7. Barzahlung durch Zahlungsanweisung. Hat der ZahlungsempfĂ€nger kein Scheckkonto beim Postsparkassenamt oder ist die Barzahlung ausdrĂŒcklich beantragt worden, so versieht das Postsparkassenamt bei Zahlungen innerhalb Ăsterreichs den Scheck mit dem Auszahlungsauftrag und sendet ihn samt dem Abschnitt fĂŒr den EmpfĂ€nger als Zahlungsanweisung an das zustĂ€ndige Postamt.
8. Mit diesen Scheckvordrucken kĂ¶nnen auch Zahlungen nach dem Ausland angewiesen werden. Wird der Betrag in FremdwĂ€hrung angegeben, so ist der Aufdruck âSâ und âgâ entsprechend zu Ă€ndern und die WĂ€hrungsbezeichnung in der fĂŒr die Betragsangabe in Buchstaben bestimmten Schraffenzeile voll auszuschreiben.
9. Verdorbene Scheckvordrucke hat der Kontoinhaber zu vernichten.

pages.transcription:
Frau A n n a  M e i n g a s t
---------------------------
Linzer Gasse 16, Salzburg.

Frau Anna Meingast, Meine frĂŒhere SekretĂ€rin ist von mir er-
mĂ€chtigt, fĂŒr mich einlangende Sendungen zu ĂŒber-
nehmen und Zahlungen entgegenzunehmen oder zu lei-
sten, und habe ich dieselbe auch der Post gegen-
ĂŒber legitimiert.

Stefan Zweig

Erneuert fĂŒr Salzburg, 1. Januar 1936.
pages.notes: Das Dokument besteht aus einem maschinengeschriebenen Text, der handschriftlich durch die Anrede 'Frau Anna Meingast,' am Zeilenanfang ergĂ€nzt wurde. Die Unterschrift 'Stefan Zweig' ist ebenfalls handschriftlich.
RĂŒckseite des Dokuments, keine Beschriftung erkennbar (Durchdruck der Vorderseite).

objects.confidenceNotes: Der Text ist klar lesbar, die handschriftlichen ErgĂ€nzungen sind eindeutig Stefan Zweig zuzuordnen und gut lesbar.

pages.transcription:
[Printed by authority of the Registrar General.]
CERTIFIED COPY of an ENTRY OF MARRIAGE
Pursuant to the Marriage Act 1949
TC 574271
M. Cert. S.R.
Registration District Bath
1939. Marriage solemnized at The Register Office in the
District of Bath in the County Borough of Bath.
Columns: 1 When married | 2 Name and surname | 3 Age | 4 Condition | 5 Rank or profession | 6 Residence at the time of marriage | 7 Father's name and surname | 8 Rank or profession of father
138 Sixth September 1939 | Stefan Zweig | 54 years | The divorced husband of Frederike Maria Zweig formerly Burger | Author | Lansdown Lodge Lansdown Road Bath | Moritz Zweig (deceased) | Textile manufacturer
| Elisabet Charlotte Altmann | 31 years | Spinster | Author's Secretary | Lansdown Lodge Lansdown Road Bath | Joseph Georg Altmann (deceased) | Hardware merchant
Married in the Register Office by licence before me,
D Clegg Registrar
E G Button Supt. Registrar
This marriage was solemnized between us, Stefan Zweig, Elisabet Charlotte Altmann in the presence of us, Arthur I. Ingram, Johanna Altmann
Certified to be a true copy of an entry in a register in my custody,
[Unterschrift] Superintendent Registrar
CAUTION:âAny person who (i) falsifies any of the particulars on this certificate, or (2) uses a falsified certificate, knowing it to be false, is liable to prosecution.
3rd September 1980
pages.notes: Das Dokument ist ein offizielles Formular. Die handschriftlichen EintrĂ€ge sind gut lesbar. Ein Stempelabdruck ist im Bereich der Unterschrift des Superintendent Registrar vorhanden.

pages.transcription:
Home Office No. Z.33.
Certificate No. AZ 15747

BRITISH NATIONALITY AND STATUS OF ALIENS ACT, 1914.

CERTIFICATE OF NATURALIZATION

Whereas Stefan Zweig
has applied to one of His Majesty's Principal Secretaries of State for a Certificate
of Naturalization, alleging with respect to him self the particulars set out below,
and has satisfied him that the conditions laid down in the above mentioned Act for
the grant of a Certificate of Naturalization are fulfilled in his case:
Now, therefore, in pursuance of the powers conferred on him by the said Act,
the Secretary of State grants to the said
Stefan Zweig
this Certificate of Naturalization, and declares that upon taking the Oath of Allegiance
within the time and in the manner required by the regulations made in that behalf he
shall, subject to the provisions of the said Act, be entitled to all political and other
rights powers and privileges, and be subject to all obligations duties and liabilities, to
which a natural-born British subject is entitled or subject, and have to all intents and
purposes the status of a natural-born British subject.

In witness whereof I have hereto subscribed my name this 12th day of
March, 1940.

[Unterschrift]
Under Secretary of State.

HOME OFFICE,
LONDON.

PARTICULARS RELATING TO APPLICANT.

Full Name: Stefan ZWEIG.
Address: 49, Hallam Street, W.1.
Trade or occupation: Author.
Place and date of birth: Vienna, Austria. 28th November, 1881.
Nationality: Austrian.
Single, Married, etc.: Married.
Name of wife or husband: Elizabet Charlotte.
Names and nationality of parents: Moritz and Ida ZWEIG. Austrian.
(For Oath
see overleaf.)
pages.notes: Die Unterschrift unter 'Under Secretary of State' ist eine Faksimile-Signatur (Maxwell). Das Dokument enthĂ€lt maschinenschriftliche ErgĂ€nzungen in den Formularfeldern.

V e r t r a g

Â§ 1.

Durch VerfĂŒgung des Herrn RegierungsprĂ€sidenten in Aussig vom
28 Juni 1940, Aktenzeichen III A-Wi/Jd.IV/8 wurde der gefertig-
te Rechtsanwalt Dr. jur. Erhard J u s t in Reichenberg als
TreuhĂ€nder fĂŒr die VerĂ€usserung der den Juden Alfred Zweig und
Dr. Stefan Zweig, derzeit unbekannten Aufenthaltes, gehĂ¶rigen
Aktien der Firma M. Zweig mechanische Weberei A G. in Ober-Ro-
senthal bei Reichenberg bestellt.

Der bestellte TreuhĂ€nder hat demzufolge mit der zugelassenen
Erwerberin, der Firma Vereinigte FĂ€rbereien Aktiengesellschaft,
Reichenberg, nachstehenden
V e r t r a g
abgeschlossen.

Â§ 2.

Das Grundkapital der Firma M. Zweig mechanische Weberei A.G. in
Ober Rosenthal bei Reichenberg betrĂ€gt K 7,000.000.--, zerlegt
in 17.500 StĂŒck auf den Inhaber lautende Aktien von je K 400.--.
Im Eigentum der VerĂ€usserer stehen 15.750 StĂŒck, erliegend im
Depot der BĂ¶hmischen Escompte-Bank ( frĂŒher Bank fĂŒr Handel und
Industrie, ehemals LĂ€nderbank ), Prag.

Â§ 3.

Die durch den bestellten TreuhĂ€nder vertretenen Alfred Zweig
und Dr.Stefan Zweig verkaufen und ĂŒbergeben nun die Firma
Vereinigte FĂ€rbereien Aktiengesellschaft, Reichenberg 15.750
StĂŒck Aktien der Firma M. Zweig mechanische Weberei A.G. in
Ober Rosenthal bei Reichenberg zum Kaufpreise von RM 908.951.66,
in Worten: Reichsmark neunhundertachttausendneunhunderteinund-
fĂŒnfzig 66/100. Der Preis wurde festgestellt auf Grund des ĂŒber
behĂ¶rdlichen Auftrag angefertigten Wertgutachtens der Deutschen

WirtschaftsprĂŒfungs- und Treuhandgesellschaft m.b.H., Berlin-
Reichenberg vom 14.10.1939.

Â§ 4.

Die Uebergabe der Aktienurkunden hat mit Dividendenschein ab
1938 zu erfolgen.

Der bestellte VerkaufstreuhĂ€nder erklĂ€rt im Namen der von ihm
vertretenen VerĂ€usserer seine Einwilligung, dass aus dem Depot
der BĂ¶hmischen Escompte-Bank ( Bank fĂŒr Handel und Industrie,
ehemals LĂ€nderbank ), Prag, die kaufgegenstĂ€ndlichen 15.750 S
StĂŒck Aktien der Firma M. Zweig mechanische Webereien A.G., Ober
Rosenthal bei Reichenberg, behoben und an die KĂ€uferin ausge-
folgt werden.

Â§ 5.

Die VerkĂ€ufer welche gleichzeitig Mitglieder des Vorstandes
(Verwaltungsrates) der Firma M. Zweig, mechanische Weberei A.G.,
Ober Rosenthal bei Reichenberg waren, versichern hiemit, dass
sie ĂŒber das VermĂ¶gen der Gesellschaft seit dem 31./10.1938
nicht verfĂŒgt haben und sie daher fĂŒr die Richtigkeit und
VollstĂ€ndigkeit des VermĂ¶gens der Gesellschaft laut den Fest-
stellungen des PrĂŒfungsberichtes der Deutschen WirtschaftsprĂŒ-
fungs- und Treuhandgesellschaft m.b.H., Berlin-Reichenberg vom
14.10.1939 einstehen.

Die VerkĂ€ufer stehen weiter dafĂŒr ein, dass der Aktiengesell-
schaft die Kursausgleichs- und ZAV-BetrĂ€ge, die ihr zugeflos-
sen sind, verbleiben und nicht zurĂŒckgefordert werden und dass
die noch nicht liquidierten BetrĂ€ge aus diesem Titel in Zukunft
eingehen.

Im anderen Falle ist die KĂ€uferin berechtigt, die entsprechen-
den BetrĂ€ge von dem Kaufpreise abzusetzen, bezw. die VerkĂ€u-
fer auf GewĂ€hrleistung in Anspruch zu nehmen.

Â§ 6.

Der Kaufpreis ist von der KĂ€uferin zu Gunsten der VerkĂ€ufer auf
ein Konto bei einer Devisenbank mit Genehmigung des Herrn Ober-
finanzprĂ€sidenten, Devisenstelle in Karlsbad, einzuzahlen und
zwar zu einem Drittel binnen 30 Tagen nach Uebergabe der Aktien
und mit dem Rest in zwei gleichen Raten nach je weiteren sechs
Monaten.

Â§ 7.

Die durch diesen Vertrag auszulĂ¶sende Urkundensteuer und Kapi-
talverkehrssteuern werden von beiden Vertragsteilen je zur HĂ€lf-
tegetragen.

Desgleichen:tragen die Vertragsteile je zur HĂ€lfte die durch die
Vereinigung aller Aktien in der Hand der KĂ€uferin veranlasste
Grunderwerbssteuer.

Die Kosten des bestellten VerkaufstreuhĂ€nders gehen zu Lasten
der VerkĂ€uferin.

Urkund dessen die gehĂ¶rigen Fertigungen.

Reichenberg am 17.Juli 1940.

Dr. Erhard Just m.p.

Vereinigte FĂ€rbereien
Aktiengesellschaft
Erben m.p. pp. Buchelt m.p.

pages.transcription:
Nr. der Urkundenrolle 799/1940.

Der mir persĂ¶nlich bekannte Herr JU Dr. Erhard Just, Rechtsanwalt
in Reichenberg, Wienerstrasse Nr. 15, hat heute vor mir vor-
stehende Urkunde eigenhĂ€ndig unterschrieben.-Reichenberg, am
achtzehnten Juli eintausendneunhundertvierzig. ------------------
GebĂŒhr: 3.60 RM

Stempel
JU Dr. Robert Brosche
Notar in Reichenberg
Dr. Brosche m.p.
Notar

Genehmigt nach Â§ 1 der Anordnung auf Grund der Verordnung
ĂŒber die Anmeldung des VermĂ¶gens von Juden vom 25.4.1938
(RGB1.I S.415) und nach Â§ 8 Absatz 1 der Verordnung ĂŒber den
Einsatz des jĂŒdischen VermĂ¶gens vom 3. Dezember 1938 (RGB1.I
S.1709) mit folgenden Auflagen:
1.) Der KĂ€ufer hat fĂŒr die LĂ¶schung der jĂŒdischen Firma im
Handelsregister Sorge zu tragen.
2.) Der Kaufpreis ist durch die Hand des VerkaufstreuhĂ€nders
auf ein Sperrkonto der jĂŒdischen VerĂ€usserer einzuzahlen.
Vorher sind die nicht ĂŒbernommenen Forderungen der GlĂ€u-
biger und Ă¶ffentlichen Stellen und KĂ¶rperschaften in An-
rechnung auf den Kaufpreis zu befriedigen.
3.) Die KĂ€uferin ist nicht berechtigt, fĂŒr Kursausgleichs-
und Z.A.V. BetrĂ€ge, die in dem Wertgutachten der Deut-
schen Wirtschafts-PrĂŒfungs und Treuhandgesellschaft in
Berlin vom 14.Oktober 1939 nicht aktiviert sind, einen
Abzug vom Kaufpreis zu machen.
4.) Die KĂ€uferin hat einen zeitgerechten, der GrĂ¶sse der Ge-
folgschaft entsprechenden Gefolgschaftsraum zu erstel-
len. Eine Trinkwasseranlage ist in allen Abteilungen ein-
zurichten. Eine genĂŒgende Anzahl von Aborten mit Wasser-
spĂŒlung ist zu schaffen. Ausreichende Wascheinrichtungen
und Kleiderablagen (EinzelkleiderschrĂ€nke) sind einzu-
bauen. FĂŒr die angeordneten Massnahmen sind mindestens
86.800.-- RM aufzuwenden. Der Nachweis der ErfĂŒllung die-
ser Auflage ist spĂ€testens ein Jahr nach Beendigung des
Krieges dem Gewerbeaufsichtsamt in Reichenberg zu erbrin-
gen, das im ĂŒbrigen die DurchfĂŒhrung der Auflage zu ĂŒber-
wachen hat.
5.) Die aus dem Gewinn wĂ€hrend der Zeit der kommissarischen
Leitung zu zahlenden Steuern gehen zu Lasten der KĂ€uferin.

Der RegierungsprĂ€sident
Im Auftrage:
